source: iConnections Global Alts 2026 — AI, Tesla, defense, energy — what comes next
summary: Token efficiency is the key competitive axis for AI models — Anthropic leads on this metric
summary_extended: Baker argues that the two critical competitive variables in AI model economics are cost-per-token and token efficiency, and that the labs which optimize both will win economically over time. He specifically highlights Anthropic as a leader in token efficiency, claiming it can produce comparable-quality answers to Google using roughly half the tokens — a structural cost advantage that compounds at scale. He also cites xAI as a leader in cost-per-token due to superior data center operations, suggesting these two labs are best positioned on the economic axis of AI competition. The investment implication is that model quality alone is insufficient; the labs that deliver the best output per dollar of compute will capture enterprise and consumer spend. Neither Anthropic nor xAI is publicly listed, so there is no direct US-listed equity play implied by this thesis.
detail: Baker argues that the sum of cost-per-token and token-efficiency is the critical competitive variable. Anthropic can produce comparable quality answers to Google using half the tokens. Combined with xAI's low cost-per-token from superior data center operations, these two metrics together determine which labs win economically.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as a structural thesis with specific mechanics, not a passing observation. He returns to it multiple times.
quote_fragment: token efficiency and cost per token is going to be really really important
